title: HTTP Response Message
description: Schema describing the structure of an HTTP response message as defined in RFC 9110 (HTTP Semantics) and RFC 9112 (HTTP/1.1).

httpVersion.description: The HTTP version used for the response message (RFC 9112 Section 2.3).

statusCode.description: The three-digit integer status code indicating the result of the request (RFC 9110 Section 15).

reasonPhrase.description: A textual description associated with the status code (RFC 9112 Section 4). Primarily for human consumption in HTTP/1.1; absent in HTTP/2 and HTTP/3.

headers.description: HTTP header fields that convey additional information about the response or the server (RFC 9110 Section 6.3).

Content-Type.description: The media type of the response body (RFC 9110 Section 8.3).

Content-Length.description: The size of the response body in bytes (RFC 9110 Section 8.6).

Cache-Control.description: Directives for caching mechanisms in the response chain (RFC 9111 Section 5.2).

ETag.description: An opaque validator for the current representation of the target resource (RFC 9110 Section 8.8.3).

Last-Modified.description: The date and time the resource was last modified (RFC 9110 Section 8.8.2).

Location.description: A URI reference for redirects or newly created resources (RFC 9110 Section 10.2.2).

WWW-Authenticate.description: Indicates the authentication scheme(s) and parameters applicable to the target resource (RFC 9110 Section 11.6.1).

Retry-After.description: Indicates how long the client should wait before making a follow-up request (RFC 9110 Section 10.2.3).

Age.description: The estimated time in seconds since the response was generated or validated by the origin server (RFC 9111 Section 5.1).

Vary.description: Describes the parts of the request that influenced the response content (RFC 9110 Section 12.5.5).

body.description: The message body containing the payload data of the response (RFC 9110 Section 6.4).
